structure_blocks.text: Note to reader

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: The Directive on the Management of Procurement , which took effect on May 13, 2021, replaced the following instruments:

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Contracting Policy

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Policy on Decision Making in Limiting Contractor Liability in Crown Procurement Contracts

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: The directive has subsequently been amended:

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Effective April 1, 2022, to include a new Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses that describes procedures to address the Government of Canada’s requirement that a minimum of 5% of the total value of contracts is awarded to Indigenous businesses annually;

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Effective April 1, 2023, to integrate human rights, the environment, social and corporate governance, supply chain transparency principles, and Public Services and Procurement Canada’s Code of Conduct for Procurement into all government procurements;

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Effective June 30, 2023, to add requirements for risk-based systems of internal control, information management and proactive publication of contracts, and to reflect that the Guidelines on the Proactive Disclosure of Contracts have been updated and renamed Guide to the Proactive Publication of Contracts ;

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Effective May 29, 2024, to include new requirements related to values and ethics, documentation and reporting, as set out in:

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Appendix D.6: Ineligibility and Suspension Policy (Responsible department: Public Services and Procurement Canada); and

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Appendix F: Mandatory Procedures for Business Owners When Procuring Professional Services.

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Effective December 4, 2025, to reflect Public Services and Procurement Canada’s exclusive authority for the procurement of goods, services and construction, further to amendments to the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act ;

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Effective December 15, 2025, to increase most contracting limits in "Appendix A: Contracting Approvals" to reflect inflation;

structure_blocks.heading: Note to reader
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Note to reader
structure_blocks.text: Effective December 16, 2025, to reflect the Buy Canadian Procurement Policy Framework, under the responsibility of Public Services and Procurement Canada.

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3. Objectives and expected results

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.1 The objective of this directive is that procurement of goods, services and construction obtains the necessary assets and services that support the delivery of programs and services to Canadians, while ensuring best value to the Crown.

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.2 The expected results of this directive are as follows:

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.2.1 Procurements are managed in a manner that enables operational outcomes and demonstrates sound stewardship and best value consistent with the Government of Canada’s socio-economic and environmental objectives;

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.2.2 Procurement decisions are based on risk management practices, performance information and an assessment of full life-cycle costs whenever possible;

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.2.3 Effective governance and oversight mechanisms are in place to support the management of procurement;

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.2.4 Opportunities for collaboration are considered in procurement decisions;

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.2.5 Workforce capacity for the management of procurement is developed, maintained and commensurate with organizational need; and

structure_blocks.heading: 3. Objectives and expected results
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
3. Objectives and expected results
structure_blocks.text: 3.2.6 Actions related to the management of procurement are fair, open and transparent, and meet public expectations in matters of prudence and probity.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4. Requirements

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Senior designated officials for the management of procurement

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1 Senior designated officials for the management of procurement are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.1 Establishing, implementing, and maintaining a departmental procurement management framework, consisting of processes, systems and controls;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2 Ensuring that the departmental procurement management framework:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.1 Includes oversight, planning and reporting mechanisms including mechanisms pertaining to contracts awarded to Indigenous businesses as stated in Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.2 Includes clearly defined roles, responsibilities and accountabilities for the various governance committees involved;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.3 Incorporates performance results, lessons learned and best practices to inform procurement decision-making;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.4 Maintains the integrity of the procurement process;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.5 Contributes the procurement perspective to departmental planning functions and ensures that procurement strategies in the investment plan are in accordance with the Policy on the Planning and Management of Investments , Appendix A: Mandatory Procedures for Investment Plans;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.6 Ensures that procurement-planning reflects a strategic approach to departmental procurements, including contractual arrangements;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.7 Considers pricing strategies that enable coordinated asset management decisions;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.8 Considers input from key stakeholders, including but not limited to real property, materiel, project management, information technology, finance, security, legal services and human resources;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.9 Is commensurate to the value, risk and complexity of the procurement (or procurements) undertaken;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.10 Promotes collaborative, innovative, iterative and outcomes-based procurement approaches where appropriate;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.11 Facilitates compliance with all legal obligations such as those arising from modern treaties (such as Comprehensive Land Claim Agreements, refer to Appendix D: Requirements Included in Other Policies, Programs and Agreements That Impact Procurements, section D.1), trade agreements (refer to Appendix D: Requirements Included in Other Policies, Programs and Agreements That Impact Procurements, section D.2), official languages, accessibility, financial management, privacy, environmental sustainability and security legislation;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.12 Gives precedence to competition in accordance with the Government Contracts Regulations and trade agreements;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.13 Facilitates the collection and publication on the Open Government portal of accurate, timely and complete data for corporate reporting on procurement as described in Appendix C: Mandatory Procedures for the Proactive Publication of Contract Information and Reporting;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.14 Integrates respect for human rights, the environment, and social and responsible corporate behaviour into procurements by including measures to identify, mitigate and disclose risk that human trafficking, forced or child labour, or any other unethical business practice is occurring in departmental supply chains;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.15 Includes a risk-based system of internal controls that are maintained, monitored, and reviewed to provide reasonable assurance that procurement transactions are carried out in accordance with the framework, and applicable laws, regulations, and policies; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.16 Includes risk-based internal controls to ensure the accuracy, completeness and timely proactive publishing of information on contracts over $10,000.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.17 Incorporates, when procuring under a delegated authority from the Minister responsible for Public Services and Procurement Canada:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.17.1 Practices, procedures, reporting, and minimum training requirements to comply with any terms and conditions that may be established in their delegation instrument; and,

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.2.17.2 Direction, systems, policies, tools, templates, procedures and guidance prescribed by Public Services Procurement Canada for use by departments.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.3 Facilitating collaboration between contracting authorities and business owners throughout the investment-planning process and procurement life cycle to enable informed procurement decisions;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.4 Supporting improvement of government-wide procurement tools and practices by collaborating with Public Services and Procurement Canada, Shared Services Canada and the Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.5 Providing advice to the deputy head on:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.5.1 The nature, structure and required resourcing for implementation of the department’s procurement management function;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.5.2 Significant gaps in performance or issues of non-compliance with the requirements of this directive; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.5.3 Departmental procurement plans;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.6 Identifying and addressing the department’s needs with respect to the necessary competencies, capacity and professional development in procurement management.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.7 When the department has awarded per diem–based professional service contracts for business services, informatic services, management consulting services, or temporary help services in the previous fiscal year totaling in excess of $5 million, provide a report to the deputy head twice a year that identifies:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.7.1 The total current number of per diem–based professional service resources; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.1.7.2 The current number of per diem–based professional service resources that have been on contract with the department for a period greater than two years.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Business owners

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2 Business owners are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.1 Clearly defining, to contracting authorities, the intended outcomes of the procurement, including the operational requirements, end user needs, expected benefits, alignment with the government’s strategic direction and total costs over the life cycle whenever possible;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.2 Adhering to the Values and Ethics Code for the Public Sector and the Directive on Conflict of Interest at all times during the procurement process, including but not limited to when engaging with suppliers, evaluating bids, awarding and managing contracts;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.3 In collaboration with contracting authorities, supporting procurement-planning and decision-making by:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.3.1 Conducting market analysis to better understand industry capacity and availability; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.3.2 Establishing and maintaining valid cost estimates;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.3.3 Identifying, mitigating and disclosing occurrences or risk that may have a negative impact on human rights, and environmental and social considerations;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.3.4 Developing clear statements of work and technical evaluation criteria; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.3.5 Adhering to Appendix F: Mandatory Procedures for Business Owners When Procuring Professional Services if considering procuring professional services;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.4 Adhering to the required governance processes for projects, assets and procurement decisions, commensurate to the risk and complexity of the procurement and in accordance with the departmental procurement management framework;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.5 Engaging with contracting authorities at the outset of planning investments, in order to integrate procurement considerations into the planning of programs, services and projects;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.6 Building in sufficient lead time to ensure that procurement activities and program operational requirements are met;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.7 Ensuring that the intended outcomes of the procurement are aligned with the departmental mandate and government priorities, available funds, and key socio-economic and environmental benefits:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.7.1 Where appropriate, including accessibility considerations when specifying requirements for goods, services and construction, and ensuring that deliverables incorporate accessibility features;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.7.1.1 Ensuring clear justification is documented if it is determined that accessibility considerations are not consistent with modern treaties or trade agreements, or if it is not appropriate to include them as part of commodity specifications, or if it is not possible to obtain goods, services or construction that comply;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.7.2 Where appropriate, including environmental considerations when specifying requirements for goods, services and construction and leveraging procurement practices in accordance with the Policy on Green Procurement and documenting files accordingly;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.7.3 Where appropriate, considering opportunities to support the participation of Indigenous peoples in matters related to procurement and documenting files accordingly;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.7.4 Where appropriate, including human rights considerations throughout the procurement process, to prevent and reduce risks of unethical business practices in markets where risks are the highest, and to ensure that suppliers that do business with the government operate in a socially responsible manner; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.7.5 Where required and as appropriate, considering opportunities to support Canadian economic interests, and documenting this accordingly.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.8 In collaboration with contracting authorities, monitoring the delivery of the procurement and supporting any related reporting requirements;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.9 Consulting with Treasury Board of Canada Secretariat (and other impacted government departments) to determine requirements for seeking Cabinet or Treasury Board approval when:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.9.1 Developing or leading a program or policy that uses procurement to advance a government-wide socio-economic or environmental objective;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.9.2 Imposing any mandatory requirements that involve changes to the administration of procurement by other departments; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.9.3 Ensuring that these requests include:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.9.3.1 A clear alignment of intended outcomes with government priorities and other programs or policies; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.2.9.3.2 A sound business case, including a risk assessment, expected benefits indicators, and supporting guidance and tools for government-wide implementation.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Contracting authorities

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3 Contracting authorities are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.1 Conducting procurements on behalf of the department or agency, and establishing contracts and contractual arrangements based on sound procurement principles, including fairness, openness and transparency to obtain best value;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.2 Ensuring that the integrity of the procurement process is maintained throughout by:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.2.1 Adhering to the Values and Ethics Code for the Public Sector and the Directive on Conflict of Interest ; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.2.2 Monitoring, preventing, identifying and reporting any conflict of interest that may exist and taking appropriate mitigating action as required;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.3 In collaboration with business owners, ensuring that pricing strategies are considered in procurement-planning and throughout the procurement life cycle;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4 Providing advice and recommending options to business owners on procurement strategies that meet operational requirements and have the intended results; the procurement strategies are based on risk, complexity and best value considerations, such as but not limited to:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.1 Including outcomes-based, iterative approaches where appropriate;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.2 Incorporating market analysis and supplier engagements;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.3 Including an initial contract period with option years, off-ramps, phases, and gates to allow flexibility as appropriate;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.4 Including supplier incentives, Crown payment credits, and adjustments to contracts, their duration or length, and their processes provided that compliance, safety, quality and other specified considerations within the contract are met;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.5 Considering opportunities for the unbundling of requirements where applicable, and inviting bids by commodity to permit smaller and more specialized firms to bid;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.6 Ensuring that all decisions related to the legitimate division of requirements into multiple smaller requirements are documented, and are not intended to avoid financial approval thresholds or policy requirements, such as contract entry limits, regulatory rules and trade agreement obligations;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.7 Ensuring strategic category management, including socio-economic and environmental considerations;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.8 Using pricing strategies, including the basis of payment, incentives and performance adjustments;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.9 Considering input from relevant stakeholders responsible for investment management, project management, materiel management and real property management;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.10 Including the life-cycle costs of the asset and all stages of the procurement;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.11 Using common service providers;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.12 Using Public Services and Procurement Canada’s published list of acquisition instruments, including the mandatory standing offers and supply arrangements as listed in Appendix A: Contracting Approvals, section A.6 (Standing offers and supply arrangements);

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.13 Considering non-mandatory Public Services Procurement Canada acquisition instruments, where practicable;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.14 Applying lessons learned from previous procurements and best practices; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.4.15 Leveraging procurement to support Canadian economic interests.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.5 Implementing the departmental procurement management framework established by senior designated officials while liaising horizontally across business lines to identify opportunities for efficiencies and achieve intended outcomes;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.6 Collaborating with other government departments on joint procurements as appropriate;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.7 In consultation with business owners, monitoring, documenting, investigating and discussing contractor performance issues as they arise over the course of the contract in order to select appropriate measures required to address documented issues; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.8 Ensuring that requirements included in other policies, programs and agreements are considered and met as described in Appendix D: Requirements Included in Other Policies, Programs and Agreements That Impact Procurements;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.3.9 Incorporating the Code of Conduct for Procurement into procurements (refer to Annex D6: Code of Conduct for Procurement).

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Industry engagement and market analysis

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4 When conducting industry engagement, as appropriate, contracting authorities, in collaboration with business owners, are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.1 Assessing whether an outcomes-based procurement approach is appropriate, such as in situations where:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.1.1 There is no existing solution on the market;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.1.2 The business requirement needs cannot be met without significant modifications to existing solutions;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.1.3 The business requirement is complex and involves multiple stakeholders; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.1.4 Collaboration with suppliers in solution development may be required;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.2 Developing industry engagement strategies that respect the principles of a fair, open and transparent procurement process;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.3 Communicating the rules of that engagement with all stakeholders involved;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.4 Conducting market analysis in the procurement-planning phase to better understand industry capacity and availability;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.5 Developing performance-based evaluation criteria that are commensurate with the risk and complexity of the procurement, through the following measures, including but not limited to:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.5.1 Requesting third-party reviews and analysis;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.5.2 Organizing or participating in events, including bidders’ conferences, supplier or industry days and site visits; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.5.3 Issuing requests for information, request for expressions of interest, or advance contract award notices as required;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.6 Ensuring that there are opportunities for suppliers to collaborate and develop solutions, including pilots and prototypes, where appropriate;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.7 Leveraging market analysis and applying business acumen and negotiation skills to develop the terms of a contract; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.4.8 Revalidating the market analysis sufficiently in advance of negotiating the extension of non-competitive procurements to allow for the consideration of other options as appropriate.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Solicitation and bid evaluation

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5 Contracting authorities are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.1 Ensuring that bids are solicited for contracts, except when one of the exceptions set out in the Government Contracts Regulations applies;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.2 Publishing on the Government of Canada–approved electronic tendering system, except when publication is not in the public interest, the following as required by applicable trade agreements:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.2.1 All notices, solicitations, invitations to tender and bid documents, unless otherwise permitted by each of the applicable trade agreements;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.2.2 Advance contract award notices identifying the intended supplier for at least 15 calendar days;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.3 Simplifying solicitation documents wherever possible to support streamlined processes;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.4 To the extent possible, taking past performance into consideration when assessing a bidder’s ability to deliver, and managing the contract accordingly;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.5 Including requirements for former public servants to self-identify in solicitations and in the resulting contract clauses of service contract documents, and informing suppliers that this information will be proactively published; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.6 Collaborating with business owners to ensure that any appropriate socio-economic and environmental requirements are achievable and measurable;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.7 Designing and conducting the bid evaluation process, financial assessment and due diligence;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.5.8 Limiting the number of mandatory technical criteria to those determined to be essential requirements in order to achieve the desired outcomes and ensure that no bid is unnecessarily disqualified.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Contracting approvals

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6 Contracting authorities are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.1 Seeking Treasury Board approval when the estimated value for a contract or contractual arrangement, including taxes, expected amendments, and follow-on contract or contractual arrangement, exceeds the limits in Appendix A: Contracting Approvals:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.1.1 Prior to entering into or amending a contract or contractual arrangement;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.1.2 Prior to amending a contract or contractual arrangement if the procurement deliverables or costs change after advance approval; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.1.3 Prior to entering or amending a contract issued against an acquisition instrument (for example, standing offer or supply arrangement) when the estimated value exceeds the limits specified in the Treasury Board–approved acquisition instrument;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.2 Where appropriate, based on procurement strategies or plan, seeking advance Treasury Board approval to enter into a contract or contractual arrangement;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.3 Ensuring that emergency conditions exist and seeking the appropriate approval prior to entering into an emergency contract or contractual arrangements in accordance with the requirements specified in Appendix A: Contracting Approvals, section A.3 (Emergency contracting limits);

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.4 Seeking ministerial approval for contracts with former public servants in accordance with Appendix A: Contracting Approvals, section A.4 (Former public servants contract approvals);

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.5 Seeking Treasury Board approval to change an approval authority in accordance with Appendix A: Contracting Approvals, section A.5 (Requesting changes to contracting approval limits); and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.6.6 Seeking Treasury Board approval to limit contractor liability or to indemnify in accordance with Appendix B: Mandatory Procedures for Limitation of Contractor Liability and Indemnification in Contracts.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Employer-employee relationships

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.7 Business owners are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.7.1 Ensuring that an employer-employee relationship does not form during the contract; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.7.2 Keeping their contracting authorities informed of the conditions or changes to the way in which the work must be performed that may create an employer-employee relationship.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.8 Contracting authorities are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.8.1 Ensuring that business owners using contracted services are aware of the risks associated with the creation of employer-employee relationships, prior to and during the performance of the contract; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.8.2 Including terms and conditions that prevent the risk of creating employer-employee relationships in the contract.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Contract management and documentation

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9 Business owners are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.1 Monitoring, documenting and certifying the delivery of goods, services and construction to ensure that the delivery meets the provisions of the contract or contractual arrangement, including such criteria as quality, standards and service levels;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.2 Monitoring and documenting costs, including confirming that the invoice is in accordance with the contract or contractual arrangement and reflects the work performed;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.3 Informing contracting authorities of contractor performance, including both the challenges and achievement of key deliverables as they arise;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.4 In collaboration with contracting authorities, communicating performance issues and consequences to the contractors as they arise, and providing contractors with an opportunity to address the issues as appropriate;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.5 In collaboration with contracting authorities, reviewing complex procurements, where appropriate to:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.5.1 Assess the appropriateness of the procurement strategy;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.5.2 Determine whether best value and the intended outcomes are being achieved (including but not limited to socio-economic and environmental outcomes);

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.5.3 Identify opportunities for improvement or change where applicable;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.5.4 Document, share, and apply lessons learned and best practices, where possible; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.5.5 Apply best practices in contract management in order to achieve the desired procurement outcomes over the acquisition life cycle;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.9.6 In collaboration with contracting authorities, providing a justification for using any of the exceptions to soliciting bids in the Government Contracts Regulations .

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10 Contracting authorities are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1 Ensuring that accurate and comprehensive procurement records applicable to the contract file are created and maintained to facilitate management oversight and audit, including but not limited to:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.1 A description of the requirement, the rationale for the procurement strategy options and the identification of decision-makers involved;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.2 A record of individual assessments, consensus evaluation, relevant decisions, approvals, communications and dates;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.3 Justification for using limited tendering, in accordance with each applicable trade agreement;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.4 Justification for using a non-competitive process, in accordance with the Government Contracts Regulations (including the sole source exception invoked, the rationale for its use, and any associated documentation);

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.5 Documentation of risk management strategies, financial assessments and ongoing price validations throughout the procurement;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.6 Any records related to the limitation of contractor liability or indemnification regardless of the dollar value of the contract;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.7 Documentation of any criteria, considerations or plans that leverage procurement to provide socio-economic and environmental benefits, including any additional known costs and planned outcomes;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.8 Justification for contracting with a former public servant that includes price substantiation, risk mitigation and cost control measures to adjust for pension or lump-sum payments;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.9 A copy of the duly executed contract or contractual arrangement;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.10 The name and title of the individual assigned to the role of business owner; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.1.11 Documentation of the invocation of a national security exception, including the approval letter signed by the appropriate assistant deputy minister, the rationale for the invocation of the national security exception, and the justification for the selected procurement strategy.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.2 Including a condition in complex procurements that prohibits the contractor from supplying goods to the government of Canada that is subject to economic sanctions imposed under the United Nations Act , the Special Economic Measures Act , or the Justice for Victims of Corrupt Foreign Officials Act and listed in Appendix D: Requirements Included in Other Policies, Programs and Agreements That Impact Procurements, section D.4 (International sanctions), subsection D.4.2;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.3 Ensuring that the roles and responsibilities of stakeholders involved throughout the procurement process are well-defined and clearly communicated;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.4 In collaboration with business owners, documenting contractor performance issues as they occur, in accordance with the escalation guidance;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.5 Monitoring and managing the procurement risks identified in the planning stage and ensuring that updates are made to reflect evolving risks throughout the procurement process;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.6 Seeking approval from the financial delegation holder prior to extending the duration of a contract or amending a contract where there is a financial impact throughout the procurement life cycle;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.7 In collaboration with business owners, assessing whether to extend or re-solicit expiring long-term contracts or contractual arrangements at least two years in advance of their expiry; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.10.8 In consultation with legal services, ensuring that the contract includes appropriate cancellation or termination clauses, such as clauses that permit cancellation or termination at critical life-cycle stages for complex procurements.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Financial interests of the Crown

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.11 Contracting authorities are responsible for the following:

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.11.1 Considering whether to obtain a financial security from bidders and contractors that is commensurate with the risks involved, including the risk of a contractor’s failure or inability to perform the work under a contract;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.11.2 Taking into account the standard industry practices in evaluating the type of financial security;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.11.3 Ensuring that any company selected to provide that financial security is licensed to provide it in the jurisdiction of the eventual contract, in accordance with the Guidance on Source Lists of Companies Licensed to Provide Surety Within Canada ;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.11.4 In collaboration with business owners, ensuring that any financial security held by the Crown is redeemed, where appropriate, when a contractor or a bidder defaults on their obligations;

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.11.5 Including options for an exit strategy at key milestones, gates or phases, and monitoring that payment milestones are not missed to avoid additional costs to the Crown; and

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: 4.11.6 Where there is knowledge of an insolvency and impending or actual bankruptcy of a contractor or bidder, ensuring that any proposed action by a business owner will not prejudice the Crown’s legal position, particularly when the bankrupt contractor resides outside Canada, and that any action taken be in accordance with the bankruptcy laws of the respective foreign country.

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
4. Requirements
structure_blocks.text: Limitation of contractor liability and indemnification

structure_blocks.heading: 4. Requirements

structure_blocks.text: 6.7 In the event of a conflict between a requirement in this directive and a requirement in any other departmental policy instrument for procurement management, with the exception of the Directive on Government Contracts, Including Real Property Leases, in the Nunavut Settlement Area , the requirement in the Directive on the Management of Procurement will apply. Appendix A: Contracting Approvals
structure_blocks.text: This appendix is mandatory as prescribed by the Treasury Board. It applies to contracting authorities as defined in the Government Contracts Regulations with the exception of contracting authorities in the Schedule to the Regulations and Commissions created pursuant to the Inquiries Act .

structure_blocks.heading: Appendix A: Contracting Approvals
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Appendix A: Contracting Approvals
structure_blocks.text: For the purposes of Appendix A: Contracting Approvals, the term “competitive” includes the publishing of an advance contract award notice.

structure_blocks.heading: Appendix A: Contracting Approvals
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Appendix A: Contracting Approvals
structure_blocks.text: The contracting limits outlined in the subsections below do not confer a legal authority to contract. Contracting authorities must ensure they have the legal authority to enter into or amend a contract or contractual arrangement, such as a valid delegation under the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act, the Shared Services Act , or as conferred or required by other legislation, as applicable.

structure_blocks.text: The Government of Canada is committed to economic reconciliation with Indigenous peoples and will contribute to improved socio-economic outcomes by increasing opportunities for First Nations, Inuit and Métis businesses through the federal procurement process.

structure_blocks.text: Requirements for verifying Indigenous businesses for the purposes of these procedures are available from Indigenous Services Canada: Mandatory minimum 5% Indigenous procurement target (sac-isc.gc.ca).

structure_blocks.heading: Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses
structure_blocks.text: This commitment also supports the requirements set out in subsection 4.1.3.6 of the Treasury Board Policy on the Planning and Management of Investments to uphold the obligations of the Crown with respect to Indigenous peoples. These obligations include Canada’s procurement obligations arising from multiple comprehensive land claims agreements (CLCAs). In the event of a conflict between a requirement in these mandatory procedures and a legal obligation, including an obligation under a CLCA, the legal obligation will apply.

structure_blocks.heading: Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses
structure_blocks.text: These procedures should also be read in conjunction with the Treasury Board Directive on Government Contracts, Including Real Property Leases, in the Nunavut Settlement Area . In the event of a conflict between a requirement in these mandatory procedures and a requirement in the Directive on Government Contracts, Including Real Property Leases, in the Nunavut Settlement Area , the requirement in the directive will apply.

structure_blocks.heading: Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses
heading_path: Directive on the Management of Procurement
Appendix E: Mandatory Procedures for Contracts Awarded to Indigenous Businesses
structure_blocks.text: Departments are to meet or exceed the 5% target no later than the fiscal year-end of 2024–25, as set out in the schedule prescribed by Indigenous Services Canada for when departments are required to meet the 5% target. This schedule is based on an assessment of departments’ levels of readiness to meet the 5% target using prior departmental procurement plans related to the Procurement Strategy for Indigenous Business .
